mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack triggers earnings recession fears that force equity valuations lower over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, or signal IC turns positive.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals broad-based consumer demand strength that drives equity prices higher over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60d returns in over 55 percent of observations, or interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth during high inflation periods triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening that compresses equity multiples over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat when INDPRO rises above its trend during CPIAUCSL readings above 210, or regime-specific IC falls below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate increases above 4.0 percent impose severe financing constraints on highly leveraged firms, causing systematic equity drawdowns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns exceed zero in more than 65 percent of periods where FEDFUNDS surpasses 4.0 percent, or threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market absorption that supports corporate revenue growth and positive equity returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline yields negative 60d returns in over 60 percent of cases, or interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth directly translates into higher corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter regardless of monetary conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 drops below 0.02 across all 3-year rolling windows, or 60-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with GDP growth direction.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production during low interest rate environments signal demand destruction rather than supply shocks, prompting flight-to-quality flows that depress broad equity indices over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns turn positive when INDPRO falls during FEDFUNDS periods below 1.0 percent, or the negative predictive power disappears in walk-forward validation.
